generated_items.name: Sugar Maple (North)
generated_items.description: A broad sugar maple anchors the north corner of the square. In autumn its canopy blazes red-orange, dropping leaves onto the brick path below. Someone has carved a heart into the bark, low down, from long ago.

generated_items.name: Granite War Memorial
generated_items.description: A chest-high block of rough-hewn granite engraved with thirty-one names — Brindlewick men who didn't come home from two wars. A small American flag, slightly faded, has been tucked into the seam at the base. Someone replaces it every few months.
generated_items.lore_note: The most recent name on the memorial is from 1971.

generated_items.name: Iron Bench
generated_items.description: A black iron bench with a slatted wood seat, worn smooth from decades of use. A faint ring on one slat marks where someone regularly sets their coffee cup.

generated_items.name: Herringbone Brick Path
generated_items.description: The square's paths are laid in old herringbone brick, worn concave at the center from a century of foot traffic. A few bricks have been replaced — you can tell by the slightly different color.

generated_items.name: Town Pigeons
generated_items.description: A small flock of pigeons has claimed the memorial as their own. They strut the brick paths with proprietary confidence, scattering only when someone walks too close.

generated_items.name: Lost Glove
generated_items.description: A single knitted glove, forest green, sitting on top of the memorial's iron fence post. Someone placed it there hopefully — the universal language of lost-and-found.

generated_items.name: Community Bulletin Board
generated_items.description: A cork board under a small wooden awning near the square's entrance. Layers of notices, half-covered by newer ones: a church bake sale, a missing cat (orange tabby, answers to Marmite), a handwritten ad for cord wood.

generated_items.name: Drift of Fallen Leaves
generated_items.description: A drift of maple leaves has gathered against the base of the memorial — amber and crimson, still bright. They crackle underfoot when the wind pushes them across the brick.

generated_items.name: Acorn
generated_items.description: A perfect acorn, cap still attached, sitting in a crack between two bricks. Small enough to overlook entirely.

generated_items.name: Penny on the Ground
generated_items.description: A penny, heads up, wedged between bricks. Someone might have left it on purpose.

generated_items.name: Old Newspaper
generated_items.description: A copy of the Brindlewick Courier, two days old, left on a bench and softened by morning dew. The front page covers the upcoming Harvest Festival. A crossword is half-finished in blue pen.

generated_items.name: Cast-Iron Planter
generated_items.description: One of four matching cast-iron planters arranged around the square. This one holds late-season marigolds, slightly leggy now, their orange vivid against the grey stone.

generated_items.name: Town Clock
generated_items.description: A post-mounted clock at the center of the square, maintained by Rosalind Webb. It keeps perfect time. The face is cream enamel with black Roman numerals, slightly foxed at the edges. A small brass plate at the base reads: 'Presented to Brindlewick by the Alderman Family, 1923.'
generated_items.lore_note: Rosalind winds the clock every Sunday morning.

generated_items.name: Pigeon Feather
generated_items.description: A small grey feather, banded white at the tip, resting against the memorial stone.

generated_items.name: Carved Initials
generated_items.description: On the underside of the bench's wooden seat, barely visible: 'E.F. + T.B. 1987.' The letters are careful and deliberate, not the hasty scrape of a penknife.

generated_items.name: Maple Seed (Samara)
generated_items.description: A paired maple seed — the kind children call a helicopter — resting on the brick. It spins when you pick it up and set it loose.

generated_items.name: Harvest Festival Banner
generated_items.description: A canvas banner strung between two lamp posts reads 'Brindlewick Harvest Festival' in painted letters. The edges are fraying slightly, which is how you know it's been in storage since last year.

generated_items.name: Paper Coffee Cup
generated_items.description: A waxed paper cup from the bakery, lid still on, left under the bench. Cold.

generated_items.name: Snow on the Memorial
generated_items.description: A thin coat of snow has settled on the memorial's flat top, softening the engraved letters. Someone has placed a small candle in a jar beside it — unlit but placed with intention.

generated_items.name: Small American Flag
generated_items.description: A small flag on a plastic stick, tucked into the base of the memorial. Slightly faded from sun exposure, but stood upright with purpose.

generated_items.name: Cast-Iron Lamp Post
generated_items.description: One of six matching lamp posts ringing the square, gas-conversion electric. The glass globe collects moths at night. Someone has stuck a small sticker on the base — a yellow sun.

generated_items.name: Spring Crocuses
generated_items.description: Purple and yellow crocuses have pushed up through the soil around the base of the maple, bright and improbable in the early spring cold.

generated_items.name: Chalk Drawing
generated_items.description: A child has drawn a hopscotch grid on the brick path in pink and blue chalk. It ends at number nine with an extra square labeled 'HOME' in wobbly capitals.

generated_items.name: Dog Water Bowl
generated_items.description: A terracotta bowl set near the bench, filled daily by someone (you suspect Dot Flowers). A few leaves float in it.

generated_items.name: Forgotten Scarf
generated_items.description: A red wool scarf draped over the back of the iron bench, forgotten after last night. It's soft, hand-knitted, and smells faintly of cedar.

generated_items.name: Memorial Dedication Plaque
generated_items.description: A smaller bronze plaque affixed below the names reads: 'In memory of those from Brindlewick who gave their lives in service of their country. Dedicated 1949 by Mayor Harold Finch.'
generated_items.lore_note: Harold Finch was Eleanor's grandfather.

generated_items.name: Dandelion
generated_items.description: A single dandelion has forced itself up between two bricks, fully flowered, yellow and defiant.

generated_items.name: Ice on the Path
generated_items.description: A patch of black ice has formed on the shaded stretch of brick path between the bench and the memorial. Someone has scattered road salt nearby but not quite here.

generated_items.name: Dropped Knitting Needle
generated_items.description: A single aluminum knitting needle, size 7, caught in a crack between bricks. Hettie Mossgrove was sitting here yesterday.

generated_items.name: Dry Fountain Basin
generated_items.description: A stone basin in the east corner of the square, clearly once a fountain. The spout is long gone; the basin collects rainwater and leaves. Someone has placed three smooth river stones inside, arranged in a line.
generated_items.lore_note: Town records suggest the fountain was removed in 1958, reason unrecorded.

generated_items.name: Smooth River Stone
generated_items.description: One of three small stones arranged in the dry fountain basin. Pale grey, perfectly oval, smoothed by water. Someone brought these here deliberately.

generated_items.name: Glass Display Case
generated_items.description: A long glass-fronted case runs the length of the counter, tiered inside with trays of pastries and bread. The glass is always slightly fogged from the heat behind it.

generated_items.name: Chalkboard Menu
generated_items.description: A large blackboard behind the counter lists the day's offerings in Marigold's neat hand. Today: sourdough, cinnamon rolls, apple galette, pumpkin muffins. Prices in small figures on the right. At the bottom: 'Special — Honey cake (one left).'

generated_items.name: Cinnamon Roll
generated_items.description: A spiral of soft dough, glazed with icing that's still slightly warm. The smell alone is worth the visit.

state_transitions.description_override: A cinnamon roll, the glaze now set and matte. Still good, but past its peak.

generated_items.name: Sourdough Loaf
generated_items.description: A round sourdough with a scored cross on top, crust deeply burnished. It makes a hollow sound when tapped.

generated_items.name: Pumpkin Muffin
generated_items.description: A large muffin with a domed top, dusted with turbinado sugar, spiced with cinnamon and something else — cardamom, maybe.

generated_items.name: Apple Galette
generated_items.description: A rustic free-form tart — thin, buttery pastry folded over thinly sliced apples, edges caramelized where the juices ran. One slice remains.

generated_items.name: The Copper Kettle
generated_items.description: The bakery's namesake: a large copper kettle that sits on a shelf above the espresso machine, clearly decorative. It has the deep patina of something very old and well-used, and a small dent on one side that no one has ever explained.
generated_items.lore_note: Marigold says her grandmother brought it from Accra. She doesn't elaborate.

generated_items.name: Espresso Machine
generated_items.description: A commercial espresso machine takes up one end of the counter, stainless steel with a worn group head. The steam wand is perpetually damp.

generated_items.name: Cup of Coffee
generated_items.description: A ceramic mug of black coffee, poured fresh. The surface holds a faint sheen.

state_transitions.description_override: A mug of coffee, now only warm. Still fine.
A mug of cold coffee. Less fine.

generated_items.name: Old Wooden Counter
generated_items.description: The bakery counter is a single slab of maple, worn to a warm glow at the customer-facing edge. There are score marks from a hundred thousand loaves along the back.

generated_items.name: Bread Knife
generated_items.description: A long serrated knife resting across a wooden block beside the display case. The handle is cracked and wrapped in electrical tape — Marigold has been meaning to replace it for two years.

generated_items.name: Kitchen Pass-Through Window
generated_items.description: A low window in the wall behind the counter looks into the commercial kitchen. You can see the outline of baking trays, flour dust in the air, and sometimes Marigold's hands moving efficiently across a worktop.

generated_items.name: Tip Jar
generated_items.description: A mason jar on the counter with a handwritten label: 'Tips :)'. Contains a collection of ones, quarters, and one Canadian dollar coin.

generated_items.name: Recipe Card Box
generated_items.description: A wooden recipe card box sits on a shelf beside the register, packed with index cards in several different handwritings. Some are laminated; some are barely legible. The box is old enough to have a small lock, no key.
generated_items.lore_note: One of the cards, near the back, is written in a language Marigold can't identify.

generated_items.name: Vase of Fresh Flowers
generated_items.description: A ceramic vase of mixed flowers sits on the counter — dahlias, one stem of aster, a sprig of something wild. Changed weekly. Currently vivid and open.

state_transitions.description_override: A vase of flowers, petals beginning to drop. Past their best.

generated_items.name: Flour-Dusted Apron
generated_items.description: A well-worn canvas apron hanging on a hook beside the kitchen door. It's been washed so many times the ties are fraying, and no amount of washing gets all the flour out of the weave.

generated_items.name: Honey Cake
generated_items.description: The last slice of Marigold's honey cake — almond-scented, dense in the best way, with a thin honey glaze that's crackled on top. Agnes Perkins's alpine honey is the secret.
generated_items.lore_note: Marigold only makes this when Agnes brings honey. Which is increasingly rare.

generated_items.name: Window Seat
generated_items.description: A narrow bench padded with a worn cushion runs along the front window, facing out to the square. It seats two comfortably, three if no one minds. There are crumbs in the corners.

generated_items.name: Small Corner Table
generated_items.description: One of three small tables crowded into the bakery's front room. This one has a wobble that a folded napkin under one leg only partially corrects.

generated_items.name: The Bakery Cat
generated_items.description: A large grey cat asleep on the window seat, wedged between the cushion and the glass. It does not appear to have moved in several hours. A handwritten note on the wall above reads: 'Please do not feed Miso.'
generated_items.lore_note: Miso has been the bakery cat for eleven years. Marigold insists she belongs to herself.

generated_items.name: Gingerbread Person
generated_items.description: A hand-decorated gingerbread figure with white icing buttons and a slightly lopsided expression. Seasonal — only when Marigold is in the mood.

generated_items.name: Flour Sack
generated_items.description: A fifty-pound paper sack of bread flour leaning against the kitchen doorframe, partially used, folded over at the top and held with a binder clip. The brand is local — a mill two towns over.

generated_items.name: Countertop Radio
generated_items.description: A small radio on a shelf near the kitchen, tuned to a station that plays soft jazz in the morning and news at noon. The antenna is bent at an angle that took Marigold a week to calibrate.

generated_items.name: Small Honey Jar
generated_items.description: A squat jar of honey on the counter beside the register, set out for customers to drizzle on their pastries. The label reads 'Perkins Alpine — Brindlewick' in hand-typed text.

generated_items.name: Torn Receipt
generated_items.description: A register receipt torn in half and left on the edge of the counter. The legible half shows: 'sourdough x2, coffee x1 — $11.50'. The other half is gone.

generated_items.name: Candle Lantern
generated_items.description: A small glass lantern on each table, with a battery-operated tea light inside. Marigold turns them on when the afternoons get dark.

generated_items.name: Apple Press
generated_items.description: A large cast-iron and hardwood press dominates one end of the cider house floor. Its wooden screw is worn smooth, the oak frame dark with decades of juice. In autumn it runs almost constantly.
generated_items.lore_note: Agnes's grandfather built this press in 1938.

generated_items.name: Row of Oak Barrels
generated_items.description: Six oak barrels in a row along the far wall, bound in iron, their staves dark with age. Each one has a chalk mark on the lid indicating the batch year. The oldest reads '2019'; the newest, this autumn.

generated_items.name: Cider Mug
generated_items.description: A heavy ceramic mug filled with fresh-pressed cider, still slightly cloudy, smelling of apples and something bright and fermented at the edge.

state_transitions.description_override: A mug of cider, gone cold. Still good, but different — sharper somehow.

generated_items.name: Bushel of Apples
generated_items.description: A wooden bushel bin overflowing with autumn apples — a mix of varieties, some with a faint blush, some nearly gold. The smell is overwhelming in the best possible way.

generated_items.name: Braeburn Apple
generated_items.description: A single apple from the bin — a Braeburn, crisp and slightly tart, with a red-gold skin. Agnes grows them on the upper slope of the orchard.

generated_items.name: Stoneware Fermentation Crock
generated_items.description: A large stoneware crock on the kitchen shelf, sealed with a cloth and rubber band. It emits a faint, yeasty smell. Agnes doesn't say what's in it.

generated_items.name: Pair of Rubber Boots
generated_items.description: A pair of green rubber boots by the door, caked with orchard mud that has dried to a fine red-brown. Agnes's, obviously — the left one has a crack near the toe sealed with duct tape.

generated_items.name: Farmhouse Kitchen Table
generated_items.description: A heavy farmhouse table in the center of the kitchen, its surface scarred with knife marks, apple stains, and the ghost of a hundred meals. There are always a few things on it — a mug, a newspaper, a half-eaten apple.

generated_items.name: Bottle of Hard Cider
generated_items.description: A dark brown bottle sealed with a swing-top, labeled 'Perkins Alpine Orchard — Dry Cider 2024' in a simple typeface. The kind of thing you'd bring as a gift to someone you respect.

generated_items.name: Pruning Shears
generated_items.description: A pair of bypass pruning shears hanging on a nail by the door, blades clean and slightly oiled. Agnes sharpens them every February.

generated_items.name: Orchard Ledger
generated_items.description: A cloth-bound ledger on the kitchen shelf tracks yield by variety and year going back to 1971. The handwriting changes twice — Agnes took over the records in 1994. Notes in the margins mention unusual frosts, a good bee year, one terrible drought.
generated_items.lore_note: The 2003 entry has a red asterisk and the single word 'Blight.'

generated_items.name: Jar of Alpine Honey
generated_items.description: A squat jar of thick pale honey from the high meadow hives above the orchard. The flavor is complex — clover and wildflower with something resinous underneath. Agnes collects it herself each July.
generated_items.lore_note: This is the honey Marigold uses for her honey cake.

generated_items.name: Bundles of Drying Herbs
generated_items.description: Several bundles of herbs — thyme, rosemary, and something darker Agnes calls 'bitter weed' — hang upside-down from a ceiling beam, tied with kitchen twine. They've been there long enough to be fully dry and beginning to crumble at the edges.

generated_items.name: Cast-Iron Woodstove
generated_items.description: A squat cast-iron stove in the kitchen corner, the kind that heats the whole downstairs if you keep it fed. The firebox door has a glass panel, and in the evenings the firelight makes the whole room amber.

generated_items.name: Mechanical Apple Peeler
generated_items.description: A hand-cranked apple peeler bolted to the edge of the table, its cast-iron gears slightly rusty. It peels, cores, and slices in one motion. Agnes uses it for bulk processing; her hands are fast enough not to need it otherwise.

generated_items.name: Orchard Calendar
generated_items.description: A paper calendar from a local feed store, hung beside the back door. The months are marked with Agnes's notations: frost dates, spray schedules, a county fair entry in September circled twice.

generated_items.name: Fallen Apple
generated_items.description: An apple that has fallen from the tree nearest the house, lying in the grass. It has one brown spot where it landed but is otherwise perfect.

generated_items.name: Pressing Cloth
generated_items.description: A square of fine-mesh cheesecloth draped over the press frame, stained amber-brown from years of cider. It smells intensely of apples.

generated_items.name: Old Family Photograph
generated_items.description: A framed black-and-white photograph on the kitchen wall shows a younger version of this farm — the house smaller, the orchard saplings. Three people stand in front of the press, squinting into the sun. One of them is Agnes's mother; you can see it in the posture.

generated_items.name: Snow Shovel
generated_items.description: A heavy-duty aluminum shovel leaning against the outside wall of the cider house, moved to the door in anticipation of winter. The blade is worn on one side from years of use.

generated_items.name: Roll of Sisal Twine
generated_items.description: A large roll of sisal twine on a spike near the door, used for tying herb bundles, trellising, and about forty other things. It's half-spent and starting to unravel at the loose end.

generated_items.name: Beekeeper's Smoker
generated_items.description: A small metal bellows-and-canister smoker used to calm bees during hive inspection. It hangs on a nail by the back door, smelling faintly of char and pine. Agnes keeps her hives on the upper slope.

generated_items.name: Card Catalogue
generated_items.description: Two oak card catalogue cabinets stand against the back wall, each with forty small drawers bearing brass pulls. They are still maintained even though the collection is digitized — Eleanor considers them a finding aid with character.

generated_items.name: Overdue Library Card
generated_items.description: A library card someone left tucked in the return slot, rather than a book. It belongs to G. Hobbs, and has not been used since 2019.
generated_items.lore_note: Gerald Hobbs hasn't been in to return his books in years.

generated_items.name: Reading Table
generated_items.description: A long oak reading table dominates the center of the main room, with eight matching chairs. Someone has left a neat stack of books at one end, a pencil placed precisely on top.

generated_items.name: Library Pencil
generated_items.description: A short yellow pencil, pre-sharpened, with 'BRINDLEWICK PUBLIC LIBRARY' stamped on its side in faded blue. They're in a cup at the circulation desk.

generated_items.name: Periodical Stacks
generated_items.description: Shelves of bound periodicals line the east wall — years of the Brindlewick Courier, the county gazette, several agricultural journals, and one complete run of a naturalist magazine from 1955 to 1988.

generated_items.name: Local History Section
generated_items.description: A dedicated corner of shelving holds books about Brindlewick and the surrounding county — town histories, family genealogies, a thin volume about the covered bridge, and a self-published memoir by a former mayor.

generated_items.name: Library Computer Terminal
generated_items.description: Two aging desktop computers on a shared table near the window, available by sign-up sheet. One has a sticky note on the monitor: 'Mouse is slow — be patient.' The other one is faster.

generated_items.name: Reading Glasses
generated_items.description: A pair of wire-rimmed reading glasses left on the circulation desk. Eleanor's spare pair — she keeps them here because she keeps leaving them at home.

generated_items.name: Unabridged Dictionary
generated_items.description: A huge dictionary on its own wooden stand near the reference section, its spine repaired twice with linen tape. The pages have that particular thickness of paper that resists tearing. It falls open naturally at the M's, for some reason.

generated_items.name: Book Return Cart
generated_items.description: A wheeled metal cart beside the circulation desk, loaded with books waiting to be reshelved. Eleanor reshelves in order of Dewey decimal; the cart is organized accordingly.

generated_items.name: Reserved Book
generated_items.description: A slim book on the hold shelf with a name tag: 'FLETCHER GRANGE'. The title is 'Thermal Stratification in Mountain Lakes.' It has been here for three weeks.

generated_items.name: Newspaper Rack
generated_items.description: A wooden rack near the entrance holds today's Brindlewick Courier, the county paper, and, improbably, a three-day-old copy of the Wall Street Journal.

generated_items.name: Potted Fern
generated_items.description: A large Boston fern on a stand near the window, thriving in the diffuse library light. Eleanor mists it on Wednesdays. It is, objectively, the best-cared-for plant in town.

generated_items.name: Brass Owl Bookends
generated_items.description: A pair of brass owl bookends holding up a row of reference books on the circulation desk. They've been there so long that the shelf has lighter patches where they used to sit before someone moved them slightly.

generated_items.name: Lost Bookmark
generated_items.description: A pressed maple leaf used as a bookmark, found in a returned book and placed on the lost-and-found tray at the desk. It's been there long enough to flatten completely.

generated_items.name: Book Donation Box
generated_items.description: A cardboard box near the entrance labeled 'Book Donations Welcome.' Currently contains three paperback romances, a water-damaged field guide, and a book on tax preparation from 2008.

generated_items.name: Staircase to Upper Level
generated_items.description: A narrow wooden staircase at the back of the room leads to the upper stacks. A small sign at the bottom reads 'Quiet Please (more than usual).' The fifth step creaks.

generated_items.name: Librarian's Nameplate
generated_items.description: A small brass nameplate on the circulation desk: 'Eleanor Finch-Hartwell, M.L.S., Head Librarian.' Someone — probably a child — has stuck a small star sticker below the name. It has been there for years.

generated_items.name: Old Typewriter
generated_items.description: A manual typewriter on a shelf behind the desk, apparently decorative now. A half-typed page is still in the carriage: 'Brindlewick Public Library Annual Report — ' and then nothing. Dated 1991.

generated_items.name: Library Globe
generated_items.description: A twelve-inch globe on a wooden stand in the corner, circa 1975 based on the country names. Someone has placed a small sticky flag on Brindlewick's approximate location.

generated_items.name: Bound Correspondence Volume
generated_items.description: One of many fat volumes of bound letters, this one labeled 'Alderman Family Correspondence 1902–1918.' The binding is cracking; Eleanor has flagged it for conservation. The letters are formal, measured, and occasionally alarming.
generated_items.lore_note: Several letters from 1911 are missing. The gaps are noted in pencil.

generated_items.name: Town Ledger 1889
generated_items.description: A thick ledger with marbled covers, the pages filled in an extremely small hand. It appears to be a record of town finances — but certain entries are crossed out with particular thoroughness.

generated_items.name: Cedar and Old Paper Scent
generated_items.description: The archive room has a particular smell — cedar from the lining of the shelves, old paper with a slight sweetness, and something else underneath that might be leather or might be time. It becomes noticeable only when you first step in.

generated_items.name: Box of Loose Photographs
generated_items.description: A shoebox-sized archival box holds loose photographs, unsorted. They span several decades. Faces look back from group portraits, picnics, storefronts, winter scenes. A few faces recur — you'd recognize them if you knew the town well.

generated_items.name: Survey Map of Brindlewick, 1892
generated_items.description: A large folded survey map in a flat archival drawer, showing the town as it was in 1892. Several properties are labeled with names no longer in use; the lake is called something different. The mill — long gone — is marked with a small drawing.
generated_items.lore_note: The map shows a structure on the south lakeshore labeled 'Boathouse (A. family).'

generated_items.name: Magnifying Loupe
generated_items.description: A small magnifying loupe on a cord, used for reading fine print in old documents. Eleanor keeps it on a hook by the door; researchers are expected to return it.

generated_items.name: Archival Cotton Gloves
generated_items.description: A pair of white cotton gloves required for handling fragile documents. A sign on the shelf reads: 'Please wear provided gloves. No exceptions. — E.F.H.'

generated_items.name: Town Founding Charter, 1843
generated_items.description: The original founding charter of Brindlewick under glass in a wooden frame, the ink still readable though brown with age. Signed by eleven men. The third signatory is 'Elias Alderman.' Someone has placed a small note beside it: 'Verified authentic, 1987.'
generated_items.lore_note: The charter grants the Alderman family first water rights from the lake. This clause was quietly rescinded in 1962.

generated_items.name: Shaft of Dust-Moted Light
generated_items.description: A narrow window near the ceiling lets in one shaft of light that crosses the room at an angle. In it, dust motes turn slowly, disturbed by your arrival. It is very quiet in here.

generated_items.name: Uncatalogued Box
generated_items.description: A cardboard archival box on the lowest shelf, labeled only '?? — see Eleanor' in someone else's handwriting. It has not been opened recently, based on the dust on its lid.

generated_items.name: Eleanor's Research Notes
generated_items.description: A legal pad on the reading table covered in Eleanor's small, precise handwriting. She's cross-referencing something between the 1892 map and the Alderman correspondence. Several entries are circled.

generated_items.name: Bound County Newspapers 1901–1910
generated_items.description: A wide bound volume of the county weekly, spine labeled in gold leaf. The paper has browned evenly; the photographs are blurry half-tones. A page has been dog-eared, then straightened — someone was interested in something here.

generated_items.name: Broken Wax Seal
generated_items.description: A fragment of old red wax seal, found loose in one of the correspondence volumes and placed in a small coin envelope. It bears part of a crest — a diagonal line and what might be the base of a letter.
generated_items.lore_note: The partial crest matches imagery associated with the Alderman family seal, though Eleanor has not confirmed this.

generated_items.name: Front Desk
generated_items.description: A heavy oak front desk with a brass bell for service, a rack of room keys behind it, and a guest register open to this week's page. Teddy Birch keeps the desk clean and the register meticulous.

generated_items.name: Service Bell
generated_items.description: A small brass bell on the desk, the kind you tap with a palm. It has a satisfying ring. A handwritten card next to it reads: 'Please ring once. Twice if it's urgent. Three times only in emergencies.'

generated_items.name: Guest Register
generated_items.description: A leather-bound register open on the desk, this week's page showing four guests. The names are in different handwritings; Teddy fills in the dates in his own steady hand. One guest's address is listed as simply 'traveling.'

generated_items.name: Stone Fireplace
generated_items.description: A wide stone fireplace dominates the common room's far wall, set with a fire on cool evenings. The mantelpiece holds a row of objects: a clock, a small carved bear, a framed photograph of the inn circa 1940.

generated_items.name: Small Carved Bear
generated_items.description: A hand-carved wooden bear on the mantel, about six inches high, roughly but affectionately made. It has been there long enough that no one remembers who carved it or why.

generated_items.name: Photograph of the Inn, circa 1940
generated_items.description: A framed black-and-white photograph of the inn's facade taken from the street. The sign is different — 'Birch House' — and a man stands in the doorway who is unmistakably a younger version of Teddy's grandfather.
generated_items.lore_note: The inn has been in the Birch family since 1921.

generated_items.name: Common Room Table
generated_items.description: A round table in the center of the common room, surrounded by mismatched chairs. Someone has left a jigsaw puzzle partially assembled on one end — a covered bridge scene, approximately half done.

generated_items.name: Jigsaw Puzzle (In Progress)
generated_items.description: A 1000-piece puzzle of a covered bridge, roughly half assembled on the common room table. The box lid shows the finished image — it might be Brindlewick's own bridge. A few pieces are missing, possibly under the table.

generated_items.name: Pot of Mulled Cider
generated_items.description: A ceramic pot on the sideboard, kept warm on a small burner. Mulled cider — cinnamon, clove, orange peel. A note says 'Help yourself.' In autumn and winter this is always full.

generated_items.name: Cup of Mulled Cider
generated_items.description: A small ceramic cup of mulled cider from the common room pot. Warm, spiced, and exactly what you'd want on a cold afternoon.

state_transitions.description_override: A cup of mulled cider, now cold. The spices are still there but the warmth is gone.

generated_items.name: Room Key (No. 3)
generated_items.description: A large brass key on a wooden fob, stamped '3'. Heavy enough to discourage absent-minded pocketing.

generated_items.name: Inn Bulletin Board
generated_items.description: A corkboard behind the front desk holds local notices for guests: trail conditions, town events, a menu from the bakery. A handwritten note reads: 'Ask Teddy about the lake walk.' No further explanation.

generated_items.name: Wool Blanket
generated_items.description: A folded wool blanket draped over the arm of the fireside chair — available to whoever sits nearest the fire. It's been washed but keeps a faint smell of wood smoke.

generated_items.name: Abandoned Novel
generated_items.description: A paperback thriller left face-down on the common room table, a bookmark about two-thirds through. The cover is creased from being carried in a pocket. Whoever left it hasn't come back for it.

generated_items.name: Trail Map (Complimentary)
generated_items.description: A folded paper trail map in a plastic holder on the desk, reprinted each season with current conditions. 'Courtesy of the Lantern Post Inn and Clem Rourke, Trail Warden' is printed at the bottom.

generated_items.name: The Lantern Post Lantern
generated_items.description: The namesake lantern: a large iron lantern on a post just inside the door, electrified but designed to look like an oil lamp. It's on whenever the inn is open, which is always.

generated_items.name: Dining Room Table
generated_items.description: A long table in the dining area, set for breakfast each morning and available through the day. Mismatched salt and pepper shakers, a small vase of something from the garden.

generated_items.name: Coat Rack
generated_items.description: A wooden coat rack by the front door holds a visitor's jacket, a knitted scarf, and Teddy's own waxed canvas coat. There are enough hooks for everyone.

generated_items.name: Note Slipped Under a Door
generated_items.description: A folded piece of paper just inside the threshold of the common room — someone slid it under rather than leave it on a table. It reads: 'Don't ask Teddy about 1987. Trust me. — A friend.'

generated_items.name: Lost Umbrella
generated_items.description: A compact umbrella in the lost-and-found basket behind the desk, black, with a broken spoke on the third section. It hasn't been claimed.

generated_items.name: Mayor's Portrait Gallery
generated_items.description: A row of framed portraits lines the main corridor — every mayor since 1891, photographed or painted in descending order of formality. The most recent is a photograph; the oldest is an oil painting of a man with impressive mutton chops.
generated_items.lore_note: The 1923 portrait is noticeably smaller than the others and has been moved twice.

generated_items.name: Council Chamber Table
generated_items.description: A long oval table of dark wood in the council chamber, surrounded by twelve leather chairs of slightly different ages. A microphone sits at the head position, its cable taped to the floor.

generated_items.name: Public Records Filing Cabinets
generated_items.description: Four tall metal filing cabinets in the records office, each labeled by decade and category. Property records, building permits, meeting minutes. Everything is theoretically public; in practice, you need to know what you're looking for.

generated_items.name: American Flag on Stand
generated_items.description: A full-sized American flag on a brass stand in the corner of the council chamber. Its gold fringe is slightly dusty. A smaller state flag stands beside it.

generated_items.name: Posted Meeting Agenda
generated_items.description: A paper agenda tacked to the board outside the council chamber: 'Regular Meeting of the Brindlewick Town Council.' Items include road maintenance, a variance request on Mill Street, and under New Business: 'Lake water quality monitoring — update from F. Grange.'

generated_items.name: Clocktower Access Door
generated_items.description: A low wooden door beside the main staircase, marked 'Clocktower — Authorized Access.' A padlock hangs open on the latch; Rosalind Webb has a key.

generated_items.name: Town Seal
generated_items.description: An embossed town seal on the wall near the entrance: a mountain, a lake, a pine tree, and the motto 'Ad Montibus Lux' — Light from the Mountains. Established 1843.

generated_items.name: Rolled Property Deed
generated_items.description: A rolled document tied with red ribbon in the records office, labeled 'Lakeshore Parcel — 1891.' It appears to have been pulled recently; the dust pattern around it is disturbed.
generated_items.lore_note: The deed covers the boathouse lot and specifies Alderman family water rights.

generated_items.name: Water Cooler
generated_items.description: A plastic water cooler in the hallway, the kind with a jug on top. A sleeve of paper cups beside it has been nearly used up. Someone has taped a smiley face on the dispenser button.

generated_items.name: Official Rubber Stamp
generated_items.description: A heavy rubber stamp on the records clerk's desk: 'BRINDLEWICK — CERTIFIED TRUE COPY.' The ink is always slightly too wet. Everything official that leaves this building wears its mark.

generated_items.name: Victorian Staircase
generated_items.description: The main staircase has the good bones of the 1887 original — carved newel posts, iron balusters — updated awkwardly with fluorescent lighting installed in the 1970s. The carpet is wine-red, slightly worn at the center of each tread.

generated_items.name: Council Meeting Minutes Binder
generated_items.description: A three-ring binder on the public shelf holds the last two years of council meeting minutes. The older ones are typed; the most recent are a PDF printout. Flipping through, you notice the lake monitoring topic comes up repeatedly — and is repeatedly tabled.

generated_items.name: Clock Gear Housing
generated_items.description: At the top of the stairs, the clock's gear mechanism fills a small room — a forest of brass gears, a pendulum, counterweights on chains. It ticks with the authority of something that has never been wrong.
generated_items.lore_note: The mechanism is an 1887 Barraud & Lunds movement, one of only fourteen made.

generated_items.name: Worn Stone Steps
generated_items.description: The clocktower steps are narrow, worn concave at their centers from over a century of use. The stone is cold underfoot even in summer. There are exactly forty-three steps to the clock room.

generated_items.name: Iron Railing
generated_items.description: The original iron railing spirals up the stairwell wall, bolted into stone. It's been painted over so many times you can feel the layers. In places it's cold enough to make your hand stick slightly.

generated_items.name: Clockmaker's Toolkit
generated_items.description: A canvas roll of tools left on a step near the top — Rosalind's. Inside: watchmaker's screwdrivers, a small oil can, a jeweler's loupe, a brass pick. Everything in its place.

generated_items.name: Clocktower Maintenance Log
generated_items.description: A spiral notebook hanging on a nail near the gear housing, recording every service and adjustment since 1989. The handwriting changes once, in 2003, when Rosalind took over from her predecessor. Her entries are more detailed.
generated_items.lore_note: One entry, September 2019: 'Chime off by 3 seconds. Adjusted. Note: someone has been up here. Dust disturbed on south side of mechanism.'

generated_items.name: Pigeon Nest
generated_items.description: In a corner of the clock room, behind the pendulum housing, a pigeon has made a persistent nest despite Rosalind's ongoing efforts to discourage it. Currently occupied by two eggs.

generated_items.name: View from the Clock Window
generated_items.description: The small window in the clock room faces south, looking out over the square and the lake beyond. From here you can see the whole town at once — who is where, what lights are on, which chimneys are smoking.

generated_items.name: Old Key on a Hook
generated_items.description: A large iron key hangs on a nail near the stairwell entrance. It's too old to fit any current lock in the building — a previous key to a previous door. No one has moved it for years.

generated_items.name: Alderman Finch Statue
generated_items.description: A bronze statue of Alderman Harold Finch faces the lake from the park's center, one hand raised as if pointing toward something on the water. He has the bearing of a man accustomed to being listened to. The patina is deep green on the shaded side.
generated_items.lore_note: The statue was privately funded by the Alderman family in 1949. Eleanor has noted that Harold Finch's actual historical record is more complicated than the pose suggests.

generated_items.name: Old Wooden Dock
generated_items.description: A weathered dock extends about thirty feet into the lake, its planks grey and slightly sprung. It creaks when you walk on it. Not all the boards are equally trustworthy.

generated_items.name: Park Bench by the Water
generated_items.description: A wooden bench at the water's edge, facing the lake. The slats are weathered silver-grey. Someone has scratched a small fish into the armrest — crude but affectionate.

generated_items.name: Wildflower Patch
generated_items.description: A swath of wildflowers grows along the park's edge — goldenrod, asters, a few late coneflowers holding on. Fletcher Grange has put a small handwritten sign: 'Native planting — please don't pick.'

generated_items.name: Aster
generated_items.description: A single late-season aster, purple with a yellow center — picked from the edge of the wildflower patch where Fletcher's sign doesn't quite reach.

state_transitions.description_override: A wilting aster, its petals beginning to curl inward. Still colorful but fading.
A dried aster, petals dropped, stem still held in hand. It had a good run.

generated_items.name: Lake Ducks
generated_items.description: A group of mallards clusters near the dock, watchful and unhurried. They have learned that people near the dock sometimes have bread, which they consider only fair.

generated_items.name: Duck Feather
generated_items.description: A mallard primary feather, iridescent green at the base, floating at the water's edge. Still dry.

generated_items.name: Lake Ice
generated_items.description: The lake's edge has frozen in a thin clear sheet, extending a few feet from shore. You can see the bottom through it — pebbles and one lost boot. The ice is not thick enough to walk on.

generated_items.name: Sunset on the Lake
generated_items.description: The lake holds the last of the day's light long after the sky has gone grey, the surface briefly orange and then a deep, reflective blue. Several people from town make a point of being here for this.

generated_items.name: Tangled Fishing Line
generated_items.description: A length of monofilament fishing line tangled around one of the dock pilings, trailing into the water. A hazard to birds. Someone should cut it free.

generated_items.name: Lake-Polished Stone
generated_items.description: A flat, perfectly smooth stone from the lake's edge — grey with a faint stripe of white quartz. The water has made it almost perfect.

generated_items.name: Lakeside Park Sign
generated_items.description: A wooden sign at the park entrance: 'Brindlewick Lakeside Park — Est. 1952. Please keep the park beautiful.' Beneath it, in smaller text, a list of rules. Someone has added in marker: 'Rule 8: Be nice to the ducks.'

generated_items.name: Old Rowing Boat
generated_items.description: A wooden rowboat pulled up onto the grass beside the dock, oars inside. It has been freshly painted white and red each spring for as long as anyone can remember. There is no sign saying who it belongs to.

generated_items.name: Forgotten Observation Notebook
generated_items.description: A small waterproof notebook left on the dock bench, initials 'F.G.' on the cover. Inside: water temperature readings, dates, and notes about light patterns on the lake surface. One entry is underlined twice: 'Anomalous thermal layer — 3m. Second time this season.'

generated_items.name: Bridge Timber Beams
generated_items.description: The bridge's structural timbers are massive, hand-hewn, and dated by a carved inscription near the east entrance: '1847 — Built by hand.' They have the amber depth of wood that's had 170 years to settle.

generated_items.name: Beam Inscriptions
generated_items.description: Names and dates cut into the bridge's side beams going back well over a century — courtship carvings, travelers' marks, a few declarations of feeling. The oldest legible one reads 'R.H. 1863 — On my way south.' Several are in languages other than English.

generated_items.name: Sound of the Creek Below
generated_items.description: Through the gaps in the plank floor you can hear the creek running below — louder in spring when the snowmelt comes down off the mountain, a murmur by late summer. The darkness of the bridge interior makes the sound feel closer.

generated_items.name: Wide Plank Floor
generated_items.description: The bridge floor is wide rough-sawn planks, some original, some replaced. They flex slightly underfoot. Through the wider gaps you can see the water below, dark and moving.

generated_items.name: Weathered Work Glove
generated_items.description: A single leather work glove, dried stiff, wedged in a crack between planks. It's been there long enough to have partially composted at the fingers. No pair.

generated_items.name: Historical Marker Plaque
generated_items.description: A bronze plaque near the bridge entrance: 'Brindlewick Covered Bridge, 1847. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, 1989. Do not modify or deface.' Beneath it, in small text: 'Structural inspection due 2024.'

generated_items.name: Bat Colony
generated_items.description: In the upper rafters, invisible by day, lives a colony of little brown bats. At dusk they stream out through the openings in the siding — dozens of them, a ragged black cloud heading toward the lake. During the day you can sometimes smell them.

generated_items.name: Small Stone Arrangement
generated_items.description: Someone has arranged five flat stones in a small stack on one of the window sills of the bridge — the kind of thing people do at places that feel significant. It's been there for a while; the dust has settled around the base stones.

generated_items.name: Ice on the Creek
generated_items.description: The creek below has frozen in patches, the clear ice showing the current still moving beneath. Frost has formed crystals on the underside of the bridge planks you can see through the gaps.

generated_items.name: Wrought-Iron Cemetery Gate
generated_items.description: The gate stands open, its iron hinges rusted to a permanent position. The scrollwork at the top incorporates an hourglass, a common motif in 19th-century funerary design. The latch still works.

generated_items.name: Oldest Gravestone
generated_items.description: The oldest legible stone reads: 'Elias Alderman, Founder, 1802–1866. Beloved of this valley.' The carving is a weeping willow over an urn, the lettering still sharp. Several other Aldermans are buried nearby, forming a small dynasty in stone.
generated_items.lore_note: Elias Alderman signed the founding charter in 1843. His wife's grave is fifteen feet away and is notably less elaborate.

generated_items.name: Ancient Yew Tree
generated_items.description: A massive yew tree in the center of the cemetery, its trunk five feet around, branches spreading wide. Yews like this are often centuries old. The berries are red and intensely poisonous — a few have fallen onto the path below.

generated_items.name: Yew Berry
generated_items.description: A bright red yew berry on the path near the tree, waxy and perfect-looking. The flesh is sweet; the seed inside is lethal.
generated_items.lore_note: Do not eat.

generated_items.name: Fresh Flowers on a Grave
generated_items.description: A bunch of supermarket chrysanthemums, still in their plastic sleeve, placed on a relatively recent grave: 'Thomas Holloway, 1941–2019.' Someone has been here today.

state_transitions.description_override: Wilting chrysanthemums, petals browning, still in their plastic sleeve on the grave.

generated_items.name: Overgrown Older Section
generated_items.description: The northeast corner of the cemetery is older and less maintained, the stones at odd angles, some fallen. Grass and wildflowers have moved in. A few stones are too weathered to read; their occupants are truly forgotten.

generated_items.name: Grave Rubbing (Partial)
generated_items.description: A sheet of paper and a rubbing crayon left on a stone in the well-kept section — someone was making a grave rubbing and left mid-task. The partial impression shows a name ending in '...well' and a date from the 1890s.

generated_items.name: Stone Angel
generated_items.description: A carved stone angel stands over one of the larger plots — late Victorian, well-executed. One wing is missing, broken off at some point and never replaced. The face is serene and slightly weathered.

generated_items.name: Leaves on the Path
generated_items.description: The path through the older section is thick with fallen leaves — maples, mostly, their color gone to brown and rust. They muffle footsteps almost completely.

generated_items.name: Unmarked Stone
generated_items.description: A plain rectangular stone, no inscription, no dates. Planted in the overgrown section among the legible stones. Either the inscription wore away completely or it was never cut. Someone thought the person worth marking.

generated_items.name: Original Meeting House Pews
generated_items.description: Rows of the original 1832 pews still line the main hall, converted to museum seating. The wood is dark and smooth from generations of use. Someone has left a pamphlet on each seat: 'Welcome to Brindlewick History.'

generated_items.name: Period Furniture Display
generated_items.description: A roped-off area holds three pieces of period furniture — a Windsor chair, a drop-leaf table, a spinning wheel — each with a hand-lettered card. The spinning wheel is the real thing; the table is a reproduction.

generated_items.name: Historical Photograph Display
generated_items.description: A series of enlarged photographs along the side wall shows Brindlewick through the decades — the mill operating in 1901, the bridge construction, the first automobile on Main Street. The captions are typed on index cards, slightly faded.

generated_items.name: Artifact Display Case
generated_items.description: A glass display case holds small objects from Brindlewick's past: a hand-forged nail from the bridge, a clay pipe, three buttons, a surveyor's compass, and a folded letter under glass. Each has a handwritten label in Artie Pryce's small, neat hand.

generated_items.name: Surveyor's Compass (Display)
generated_items.description: A brass surveyor's compass in a wooden case, under glass in the display. The card reads: 'Used in the original 1843 survey of Brindlewick. Donated by the Alderman family.' The needle still moves.
generated_items.lore_note: The compass bears an Alderman family monogram on the case lid.

generated_items.name: Museum Donation Jar
generated_items.description: A mason jar near the entrance with a handwritten label: 'Donations help keep history alive — thank you!' Currently holds a collection of ones and a five.

generated_items.name: Artie's Magnifying Glass
generated_items.description: A large magnifying glass on a stand near the display case, for examining the smaller artifacts. The handle is warm from being held earlier. Artie was just here.

generated_items.name: 'A Short History of Brindlewick'
generated_items.description: A self-published booklet, 48 pages, available for $4 near the entrance. Written and printed by Artie Pryce. Covers the founding through 1980 with occasional gaps and one chapter he describes as 'disputed by certain parties.'

generated_items.name: Photograph: The Old Mill, 1901
generated_items.description: An enlarged photograph shows the mill that once stood at the end of Millpond Row — a large wooden structure over the creek, workers posed in front. The mill burned in 1927. The pond remains.

generated_items.name: Original Meeting Bell
generated_items.description: A small cast iron bell on a wooden stand near the door — used originally to call the community to meeting. It has a clear, slightly rough tone. A sign says 'Please do not ring,' which means it has been rung many times.

generated_items.name: Museum Visitors Log
generated_items.description: A spiral notebook for visitors to sign. Most entries are dates and names; a few leave comments. One: 'Found what I was looking for. Thank you, Artie.' Unsigned, dated four months ago.

generated_items.name: Glass Display Cases
generated_items.description: Three glass-topped cases run along the center of the shop, packed with small objects: watches, coins, thimbles, keys, buttons, miniature portraits, and things without obvious names. Price tags on everything.

generated_items.name: Victorian Parlor Chair
generated_items.description: A carved walnut parlor chair with burgundy velvet upholstery, sitting on a low platform near the window. Tagged: '$485 — Very good condition.' The velvet has been cleaned recently; you can see the direction of the pile.

generated_items.name: Clock Collection
generated_items.description: A shelf of clocks in various states of working — mantel clocks, a bracket clock, one pocket watch pinned open on velvet. Artie winds the working ones on Mondays. At the hour they all go off in sequence.

generated_items.name: Silver Pocket Watch
generated_items.description: A silver pocket watch in the display case, open, showing a white enamel face. Inside the case lid: an engraved 'C.H.B. 1911.' It still runs; Artie has seen to that. Tagged: '$320.'
generated_items.lore_note: C.H.B. has not been identified.

generated_items.name: Oil Painting (Lake Scene)
generated_items.description: A framed oil painting of the lake, unsigned, probably mid-20th century. The technique is competent rather than inspired, but the lake is recognizable — the particular angle of the east shore, the mountain behind. Priced at $180.

generated_items.name: Cedar Blanket Chest
generated_items.description: A cedar blanket chest against the far wall, brass hinges, key in the lock. Artie has it closed; the interior smells of cedar and old textile when you lift the lid. Tagged: '$340 — Contents not included.'

generated_items.name: Button Collection Tin
generated_items.description: A shallow tin of loose buttons in the display case — horn, bone, glass, fabric-covered, military — sold by the button or as a lot. Artie sells these to a maker in Portland who uses them in clothing.

generated_items.name: Glass Button
generated_items.description: A single glass button, slightly domed, the color of sea glass — pale green with a white shank. From the button tin. Artie sells them for fifty cents each.

generated_items.name: Stereoscope
generated_items.description: A hand-held stereoscope viewer from around 1900, with a box of stereo cards beside it — landscapes, world's fair scenes, domestic interiors. The viewer works; the images snap into 3D in a way that's still surprising.

generated_items.name: Copper Rooster Weathervane
generated_items.description: A copper weathervane rooster on a wooden display stand — a full-size original, possibly from a barn. The copper has gone deep green-brown, with streaks of brighter metal where the wind has worn it. Tagged: '$650.'

generated_items.name: Artie's Work Desk
generated_items.description: A rollup desk behind the cases, perpetually open, covered in Artie's work — research papers, a loupe, cotton gloves, a half-drunk cup of cold tea, and always something being examined or catalogued. The organized chaos of a man who knows where everything is.

generated_items.name: Cold Cup of Tea
generated_items.description: A mug of tea on Artie's desk, thoroughly cold. He starts one most mornings and rarely finishes it.

generated_items.name: Brindlewick Scrip, 1933
generated_items.description: A piece of Depression-era local scrip in the display case — a printed note for 50 cents, issued by the Brindlewick Merchants Association in 1933 when cash was scarce. Only two are known to survive. Artie has both. This one is not for sale.
generated_items.lore_note: The scrip was redeemable at seven local businesses, four of which no longer exist.

generated_items.name: Antique Key Collection
generated_items.description: A framed display of old keys mounted on cloth — skeleton keys, cabinet keys, clock keys, a few that might be safe keys. Artie has identified and labeled each one. One label reads simply: 'Purpose unknown.'

generated_items.name: Furniture Polish Cloth
generated_items.description: A soft cloth on the edge of the case, faintly scented with beeswax polish. Artie uses it constantly — the cases are always clean.

generated_items.name: Edwardian Hatpin
generated_items.description: A long hatpin with a carved ivory head, in a small glass cup on the case top. Six inches long, the pin is original — the ivory tip a stylized acorn. Artie has priced it at $28.

generated_items.name: Smell of Old Wood and Polish
generated_items.description: The shop has a particular smell — beeswax, old wood, a slight dampness from the stone foundations, and underneath everything, the faint ghost of decades of objects living together. It is oddly comforting.

generated_items.name: Stoneware Crock
generated_items.description: A large salt-glazed stoneware crock in the corner, with a cobalt blue brushstroke decoration — a simple flower. Two-gallon capacity. Used for butter, brine, or fermenting. Tagged: '$95.'

generated_items.name: Box of Paper Ephemera
generated_items.description: A flatbox of old paper items — postcards, trade cards, labels, programs — sold individually or as lots. The top layer shows a postcard of Brindlewick's main street circa 1915, addressed but never sent.

generated_items.name: Unsent Postcard, 1915
generated_items.description: A postcard from the paper ephemera box, a hand-tinted view of Brindlewick's square. On the back, in pencil: 'Dear Mother — arrived safely. The lake is very beautiful. Will write more soon. I have found something strange.' No signature. No postmark.

generated_items.name: White Gallery Walls
generated_items.description: The gallery's walls are freshly painted white, a clean neutral that makes the colors of the work pop. Small picture lights are mounted above each piece. The floor is pale maple, refinished recently.

generated_items.name: Sadie's Large Lake Painting
generated_items.description: A large canvas — four by six feet — of the lake in winter, the sky and water nearly the same grey, the distinction blurred. Sadie Mirabel's work. Not for sale. The placard says 'Lake in Second Winter' and lists no price.
generated_items.lore_note: Sadie painted this after her second winter in Brindlewick. She has declined multiple purchase offers.

generated_items.name: Current Exhibition: 'Autumn Valley'
generated_items.description: The main room holds six works by a regional painter named Holt — vivid autumn landscapes, some nearly abstract. A printed exhibition card on the door gives his bio. He's from two towns over and this is his first solo show.

generated_items.name: Bronze Seedpod Sculpture
generated_items.description: A small bronze sculpture on a plinth — an abstracted seedpod or vessel, smooth and organic. The card: 'Vessel III — local artist, bronze, edition of 5.' Priced at $420. Sadie's work, though she doesn't say so on the label.

generated_items.name: Exhibition Card
generated_items.description: A printed card for the current show: 'Autumn Valley — Works by J. Holt. October 3 – November 15. Opening reception was October 3rd. Gallery hours Thurs–Sun 11–5.' On the back, Sadie has written a note about the work in her own hand for visitors who ask.

generated_items.name: Sadie's Work Table
generated_items.description: A worktable in the back corner, visible through a half-open door. Covered in brushes, palette knives, jars of medium, and one small canvas face-down. Sadie works in the gallery when it's quiet.

generated_items.name: Historical Works Room
generated_items.description: A smaller back room holds older work — paintings of Brindlewick from the 19th and early 20th century, donated or purchased. They show the town changing: the mill, the bridge, the square before the clock. Each has an archival label.

generated_items.name: Gallery Guest Book
generated_items.description: A cloth-bound guest book near the entrance. Most visitors write their names and where they're from. One entry, from last week, reads: 'Came back to see if I imagined it. I didn't.' No name.

generated_items.name: Faint Smell of Linseed Oil
generated_items.description: Underneath the gallery's clean white smell there's linseed oil — the signature scent of oil painting, seeping from the back room. Sadie has been working this morning.

generated_items.name: Postcard Rack
generated_items.description: A wire rack near the door holds reproductions of Sadie's work as postcards — $2 each, honor system box. The most popular is the large lake painting. The second most popular is a small study of the covered bridge.

generated_items.name: Gallery Postcard
generated_items.description: A reproduction postcard of Sadie Mirabel's 'Lake in Second Winter' — the large grey painting. $2 at the honor box. A surprisingly good reproduction; the grey has depth.

generated_items.name: Lighthouse Stone Base
generated_items.description: The lighthouse base is rough-cut stone, laid in 1882 — the same local granite as the war memorial. The mortar between stones has been repointed twice. At the waterline you can see where the lake used to be higher.

generated_items.name: Metal Spiral Staircase
generated_items.description: A cast-iron spiral staircase winds up the lighthouse interior, each tread worn to a slight bow at the center. The railing is cold regardless of the season. Forty-two steps to the lamp room.

generated_items.name: Fresnel Lens
generated_items.description: The lamp housing at the top contains a Fourth-Order Fresnel lens — a beehive of concentric glass prisms that focuses the light into a beam visible for miles. It rotates electrically now; the original mechanism was clockwork. The glass is cold and perfectly clear.
generated_items.lore_note: This is one of the last original Fourth-Order Fresnel lenses still in operation on freshwater lakes in the region.

generated_items.name: Wind off the Lake
generated_items.description: At the lamp room, the wind is constant and sharp regardless of how calm it seems below. It comes straight off the water with nothing to slow it down. In winter it carries ice crystals that sting.

generated_items.name: Lighthouse Logbook
generated_items.description: A modern maintenance logbook in a waterproof case at the base of the stairs, tracking inspection dates, bulb replacements, power outages. Automated now — the entries are sparse. The oldest entries in the pre-automation section mention '...lights on the south shore, unexplained.'

generated_items.name: Old Oil Can
generated_items.description: A long-spouted brass oil can on a shelf near the base, left from the days when the lighthouse was tended by hand. It's been dry for decades but no one has removed it.

generated_items.name: View from the Lamp Room
generated_items.description: From the lamp room the lake spreads in every direction — the park and dock to the north, the town behind you, and open water to the south where the lake bends out of sight. On clear days you can see the covered bridge as a dark line over the creek.

generated_items.name: Date Stone
generated_items.description: Set into the lighthouse wall near the door: a carved date stone reading '1882 — Built for the Safety of All Who Pass.' The carving is clean; the stone around it has been weathered smooth by lake wind.

generated_items.name: Lake Lichen
generated_items.description: The south face of the lighthouse base is covered in a patchwork of grey-green lichen — crustose, extremely slow-growing. Fletcher Grange once estimated this patch to be over a hundred years old based on its diameter.

generated_items.name: 1923 Railroad Calendar
generated_items.description: A calendar on the wall, still showing October 1923, the month the rail line closed. The image is a painting of a locomotive crossing a trestle. Someone left in a hurry, or simply found it easier to stop looking at it.
generated_items.lore_note: The Brindlewick rail spur operated from 1888 to 1923. Gerald Hobbs's grandfather was the last station master.

generated_items.name: Thick Dust
generated_items.description: Everything in the house is covered in a uniform layer of fine dust — the furniture, the floors, the window sills. It has the quality of long undisturbed time. Your footprints are the only marks on the floor.

generated_items.name: Station Master's Desk
generated_items.description: A rolltop desk in the front room, closed and locked. The lock is old but solid. Through a gap in the tambour you can see papers — stacked, not scattered. Someone organized these before leaving.

generated_items.name: Nail by the Door
generated_items.description: A single large nail driven into the doorframe beside the entrance, at head height. It's been there long enough to rust in place. Whatever hung on it is gone.

generated_items.name: Old Railroad Timetable
generated_items.description: A printed timetable pinned to the wall, its paper gone to tissue softness. It shows the Brindlewick spur schedule: three trains daily, connections at the junction. The last entry is dated September 1923 with 'SUSPENDED' stamped across it in red.

generated_items.name: Broken Window
generated_items.description: One window pane is broken inward, the glass on the floor inside. The break is old — the glass has the same dust as everything else. Something small got in this way, or out.

generated_items.name: Station Ledger, 1919–1922
generated_items.description: A ledger on the window sill, its cover warped from moisture. Inside: arrival and departure times, cargo weights, passenger counts. The entries become sparse in 1922 and stop abruptly in April 1923, mid-page.
generated_items.lore_note: The last entry notes an unscheduled arrival: 'Private car — no manifest — departed 0200.'

generated_items.name: Rusted Key on Hook
generated_items.description: A single key still hangs on a hook near the desk, rusted and seized in its bow. It's the wrong shape for the desk lock — possibly for the signal box outside, which has been gone for sixty years.

generated_items.name: Bird Nest in Rafters
generated_items.description: A robin has nested in the rafters above the main room, entering through the broken window. The nest is empty now — the season is over — but still intact, woven grass and mud, precisely made.

generated_items.name: Family Photograph
generated_items.description: A framed photograph on the mantelpiece — a family posed formally, father standing, mother seated, three children. Studio photograph, probably 1910s. The youngest child holds something — too small to make out. Their faces are serious in the way of photographs then.

generated_items.name: Potbelly Stove
generated_items.description: A small potbelly stove in the corner, its pipe going up through the ceiling. Cold and dark. The ash tray at the bottom hasn't been emptied. There's still ash in it.

generated_items.name: Weatherproof Trail Map Case
generated_items.description: A green metal box on a post holds laminated copies of the current trail map. Clem replaces them when they run out or go out of date. A note at the top of the stack, in Clem's handwriting: 'Conditions: Dry. Some blowdown on upper loop. Take it easy up there.'

generated_items.name: Trail Map
generated_items.description: A laminated trail map showing the main trail system: the loop up to the warming hut, the spur to the lookout, the creek crossings. Elevations and distances marked. Clem has initialed the bottom right: 'C.R. — Oct.'

generated_items.name: Trail Register
generated_items.description: A wooden box on a post holds a spiral notebook — the trail register. Hikers are asked to sign in with date, name, and destination. Clem reads this every day. Several entries express concern about 'unusual sounds on the upper trail.' Clem's response note: 'Coyotes. Don't worry.'

generated_items.name: Trail Signpost
generated_items.description: A painted wooden signpost at the trailhead: 'Warming Hut — 3.2 mi / Brindlewick Lookout — 5.7 mi / Creek Crossing — 1.1 mi.' An arrow points up the mountain. The paint is fresh; Clem painted it this spring.

generated_items.name: Trailhead Bench
generated_items.description: A wooden bench beside the trailhead kiosk, for tying boots, resting packs, and reading the map. Clem made it himself from fallen timber. It is extremely sturdy.

generated_items.name: Trail Mud
generated_items.description: The first fifty feet of trail are churned to mud from recent foot traffic. Boot prints go in both directions; the clearest ones are very large — Clem's, going up.

generated_items.name: Trail Blaze
generated_items.description: A rectangular orange blaze painted on a tree at the trailhead — the first of many that mark the route upward. It's newer than the tree around it; Clem touched them up last season.

generated_items.name: Lost Wool Hat
generated_items.description: A dark green wool hat on the bench, forgotten. Simple and warm. The kind of thing you only notice you've lost when you're halfway up the mountain.

generated_items.name: Winter Trail Warning Sign
generated_items.description: A red sign posted seasonally: 'WINTER CONDITIONS — Snowshoes or spikes required above the warming hut. Do not attempt the lookout without proper equipment. — C. Rourke, Trail Warden'

generated_items.name: Firewood Stack
generated_items.description: A neat stack of split hardwood fills one wall of the hut — enough for several cold nights, always maintained by Clem. A handwritten sign above it: 'Please use what you need. Replace what you can.'

generated_items.name: Split Firewood Log
generated_items.description: A single piece of split maple from the stack — dense, dry, ideal. You could start a fire with this.

generated_items.name: Warming Hut Visitor Log
generated_items.description: A weatherproof notebook in a plastic sleeve hung on the wall. Hikers sign in with dates and destinations. Some leave longer notes — weather conditions, wildlife sightings. One entry from three months ago: 'Rested here during unexpected storm. Fire still good. Thank you, Clem. Whoever you are, you saved my night.' The most recent entry is today, unsigned.

generated_items.name: Builder's Plaque
generated_items.description: A small plaque beside the door: 'This hut built by Clem Rourke, 1985, for the benefit of all who walk this mountain. May you always be warm when you need to be.'

generated_items.name: Emergency Supply Kit
generated_items.description: A red plastic box mounted on the wall: emergency blanket, waterproof matches, a whistle, ibuprofen, bandages, and a small note in Clem's handwriting listing emergency contact numbers. Everything is in date.

generated_items.name: Hut Bench
generated_items.description: A single wooden bench along the inside wall, enough for three people shoulder to shoulder. Smooth from use. Someone has carved a small mountain into the seat back.

generated_items.name: Spent Matchstick
generated_items.description: A single used matchstick on the floor near the door, its head black. Someone lit a fire and then left. Recent — the char is still crisp.

generated_items.name: Snow Weight on the Roof
generated_items.description: The hut roof sags slightly under a season's accumulated snow. The structure is sound — Clem built it overbuilt — but you can hear the occasional creak and settle. Light comes in at a blue angle through a gap in the eaves.

generated_items.name: Forgotten Energy Bar
generated_items.description: A wrapped energy bar on the bench, left by a previous hiker. The flavor is chocolate-something. The date says it's still good, barely.

generated_items.name: Wind-Scoured Granite
generated_items.description: The lookout is bare granite, swept clean by wind. The rock is warm in summer sun, cold otherwise. It has the particular quality of very old stone — patient, indifferent to everything that's happened on it.

generated_items.name: Summit Marker Post
generated_items.description: A wooden post set in a cairn of stones marks the lookout: 'Brindlewick Lookout — 2,847 ft. — 5.7 mi from trailhead.' Someone has tied a faded orange ribbon to the top — a marker from a survey, or a memorial, or nothing.

generated_items.name: Panoramic View
generated_items.description: From the lookout you can see the entire valley — the lake below, Brindlewick as a cluster of rooftops and chimneys, the covered bridge as a small dark line, the fields beyond fading to hills. On clear days the next mountain range is visible, blue with distance.

generated_items.name: Summit Cairn
generated_items.description: A cairn of stacked stones marks the true summit, built up by hikers over many years. It's knee-high now, slightly wobbly at the top. A new stone has been added recently — white quartz, incongruous among the grey.

generated_items.name: White Quartz Stone
generated_items.description: A piece of white quartz placed on the summit cairn — someone carried this up specifically. It's not from the local granite. It came from somewhere else, brought with intention.

generated_items.name: Winter Panorama
generated_items.description: In winter the view is entirely different — the valley white, the lake a dark stripe, the town invisible under snow except for the smoke from chimneys rising straight up in cold still air. The silence at the top is absolute.

generated_items.name: Peregrine Feather
generated_items.description: A long flight feather, barred dark brown and cream — a peregrine falcon's primary. Peregrines hunt from this ridge. Finding a feather is rare.

generated_items.name: Portrait Gallery
generated_items.description: A corridor of oil portraits lines the main hall — Alderman patriarchs and matriarchs going back to Elias himself. Each one is slightly more formally painted than the last going backward. Constance's own portrait, the most recent, hangs at the near end and shows a woman who knows the weight of what's behind her.
generated_items.lore_note: One portrait, from approximately 1870, has had its nameplate removed.

generated_items.name: Formal Parlor
generated_items.description: The front parlor is arranged as it would have been a century ago — wingback chairs around a fireplace, a settee, occasional tables. It is immaculate. It is not quite comfortable. Constance uses it only for receiving guests she wishes to impress.

generated_items.name: Constance's Tea Service
generated_items.description: A silver tea service on a tray — pot, creamer, sugar, two cups — set out on the low table in the parlor. The tea is properly steeped; she was expecting you, or always keeps it ready.

generated_items.name: Cup of Tea
generated_items.description: A cup of Constance's tea — properly made, the real kind, with loose leaves in a strainer. Earl Grey. It's warm and the china is very good.

state_transitions.description_override: A cup of Earl Grey tea, cold now. Constance would be disappointed.

generated_items.name: Formal Garden
generated_items.description: The garden behind the house is laid out in the English formal style — box hedges, a central path, a rose border that's past its peak season now. It's been meticulously maintained, though you sense it requires constant effort.

generated_items.name: Late-Season Rose
generated_items.description: A single rose remaining on the garden bush, deep pink, slightly overblown but still whole. In a week it will be gone. You could take it without Constance noticing — though you're not sure that's quite true.

state_transitions.description_override: A rose past its prime, petals loosening at the edges. Still beautiful.
A dried rose, petals fallen. What remains has its own quality.

generated_items.name: Private Library
generated_items.description: Constance's private library is floor-to-ceiling books in a room off the main hall. They are organized, read, and annotated — this is a working library, not a decorating choice. A leather chair faces the window. This is where she actually lives.

generated_items.name: Alderman Estate Ledger
generated_items.description: A bound ledger on the library desk — accounts, estate management, property records. Constance keeps this herself, in a clear and organized hand. It goes back forty years; the earlier volumes are on the shelf behind.

generated_items.name: Alderman Family Bible
generated_items.description: A very large Bible on a stand in the library, its cover embossed with the family name. The pages at the front record births, marriages, and deaths going back to 1802. Several entries have been made in different hands over generations. One entry has been carefully removed — the page is still there, the words are not.
generated_items.lore_note: The removed entry corresponds to a gap in the 1890s family record.

generated_items.name: Silver Letter Opener
generated_items.description: A silver letter opener on the library desk, engraved with the Alderman family crest — mountain, lake, pine. The blade is sharp. It is used daily.

generated_items.name: Box Hedges
generated_items.description: The box hedges bordering the garden path are clipped to precise angles. This is not low-maintenance work. Constance pays a man from the next town to come twice a month; she does not mention this.

generated_items.name: Empty Key Hook
generated_items.description: A key rack beside the kitchen door has labeled hooks — for the garden gate, the cellar, the carriage house. One hook reads 'Boathouse' and is empty. It's been empty for some time; the hook is not rusted.

generated_items.name: Stone Walls
generated_items.description: The room's walls are rough-cut local stone, the same as the lighthouse and the oldest town buildings. Damp in places, dry in others, with a layer of whitewash applied at some point — perhaps a century ago — that has mostly flaked away.

generated_items.name: Shelved Personal Effects
generated_items.description: Floor-to-ceiling wooden shelves hold a lifetime's accumulation of objects — leather-bound books, tin boxes, folded cloth, a compass in a wooden case, small portraits. Everything is dusty. Everything is still here because someone put it here and no one has moved it since.

generated_items.name: Elias Alderman's Journal, 1858–1865
generated_items.description: A leather-bound journal, its pages brown and fragile, the entries in a small precise hand. Elias writes about the town, his family, his business concerns — and, beginning in 1862, about something else. The entries become shorter, more guarded. The last entry is dated March 1865.
generated_items.lore_note: The final entry reads: 'It must remain undisturbed. I have made arrangements. No one is to know.'

generated_items.name: Locked Tin Box
