generated_items.description: A small tin box on one of the shelves, its hasp closed with a padlock the size of a thumbnail. The tin is decorated with a floral pattern, once bright, now faded. It rattles slightly when moved — something small and loose inside.
generated_items.lore_note: No key has been found.

generated_items.name: Map Fragment
generated_items.description: A piece torn from a larger map — the lake shore, hand-drawn, with soundings marked in feet. An X is marked at a point in the southern section of the lake with the notation 'depth unknown — 1861.' The edge of the paper has been deliberately cut, not torn.
generated_items.lore_note: The rest of this map has never been found.

generated_items.name: Folded and Sealed Letter
generated_items.description: A letter folded in quarters and sealed with red wax, addressed in formal copperplate: 'To Be Opened Only By The Head of This Family Upon My Death.' The seal is unbroken. Elias Alderman died in 1866.
generated_items.lore_note: The letter has never been opened. No one seems to know it exists — until now.

generated_items.name: Founder's Compass
generated_items.description: A brass compass in a leather case on the top shelf. Similar to the one in the museum display case but older — the case is initialed 'E.A.' The needle still points north, which is somehow remarkable after all this time.
generated_items.lore_note: This is Elias Alderman's personal compass from the 1843 survey. The one in the museum is a later copy.

generated_items.name: Undisturbed Cobwebs
generated_items.description: Cobwebs connect the upper shelves to the ceiling in unbroken geometries — the kind that only form when a space is truly left alone. They billow slightly when you breathe too hard nearby.

generated_items.name: Candle Stub
generated_items.description: A candle stub in an iron holder on the shelf — white tallow, burned down to the last inch, the wick collapsed sideways into the cold wax. It was lit at some point, in a room with no electricity.

generated_items.name: Post Office Sign
generated_items.description: A metal sign above the post office door: 'United States Post Office — Brindlewick.' The flag is out, which means Dot Flowers is in. It comes down at 4:30 PM exactly.

generated_items.name: Outgoing Mail Crate
generated_items.description: A blue plastic crate outside the post office, for outgoing packages. Currently contains three boxes, two envelopes, and a rubber duck someone has mailed without explanation.

generated_items.name: Flower Shop Window Display
generated_items.description: The window of Millpond Flowers holds a seasonal display — right now: dried hydrangeas, cattails, small pumpkins, an arrangement in orange and cream. It's been in the same approximate spot for twenty years.

generated_items.name: Bunch of Sunflowers
generated_items.description: A bundle of sunflowers in a bucket outside the flower shop, heads turned slightly toward the street. $8 a bunch, on the honor system. Late in the season; these are the last ones.

state_transitions.description_override: A bunch of sunflowers, heads drooping. Past their best but still bright.

generated_items.name: Hardware Store Window
generated_items.description: The hardware store's window display changes with the season: snow shovels and salt now, garden tools in spring. A handwritten sign: 'Ask inside for special orders.'

generated_items.name: The Millpond
generated_items.description: The millpond at the end of the row is still there — a wide quiet pool fed by the same creek the covered bridge crosses. The mill is long gone, but the pond was left. Ducks, cattails, a resident heron in the early morning.

generated_items.name: Street Lamp
generated_items.description: Cast-iron street lamps line both sides of Millpond Row, matching the ones in the square. They come on at dusk automatically. In autumn the light through falling maple leaves makes the whole street glow amber.

generated_items.name: Child's Mitten
generated_items.description: A small red mitten, probably a five-year-old's, placed on top of a post near the hardware store. Someone found it on the sidewalk and put it where it might be found again.

generated_items.name: Community Noticeboard
generated_items.description: A covered board near the post office holds notices of a different kind than the square's — more personal, more local. Lost dog. Piano lessons offered. Free firewood, must haul. One note: 'Looking for anyone who remembers the Hobbs family. Ask at the post office.'

generated_items.name: Snow Shovel (Hardware Store)
generated_items.description: A display of snow shovels outside the hardware store, chained together by their handles. Ergonomic handles, aluminum blades, one heavy steel. The good ones always go first.

generated_items.name: Leaves in the Gutter
generated_items.description: The gutters along Millpond Row are choked with autumn leaves — maple, ash, a few from the big oak at the pond end. The town crew will be by with blowers at some point, but for now it's mostly leaves.

generated_items.name: Lake View from the Road
generated_items.description: Lake Street runs between a row of residential houses and a strip of parkland at the water's edge. The lake is visible through the trees — a shifting grey-blue depending on the sky. It's always there, just past the grass.

generated_items.name: Bicycle Leaned Against Fence
generated_items.description: A bicycle leaned against a white picket fence at a residential corner — unlocked, basket in front, a light that's fallen sideways. It's been here long enough that a spider has started a web between the wheel spokes.

generated_items.name: Great Blue Heron
generated_items.description: A great blue heron stands in the shallows at the edge of the parkland strip, motionless, one foot lifted. It has been there long enough that you walk past before noticing it. It does not move when you stop to look.

generated_items.name: Residential Garden
generated_items.description: The houses on the inland side of Lake Street each have a garden, and they reveal their owners: one has raised beds, still producing late kale. One is entirely ornamental and immaculate. One is a cheerful riot of whatever seeds blew in.

generated_items.name: Ice Fishing Hole
generated_items.description: Someone has cut a hole in the lake ice near the shore and left a fishing line running through it, tied to a small wooden spool. They'll be back. The hole is fresh — clean edges, no refreezing yet.

generated_items.name: Cat on a Wall
generated_items.description: A large orange cat is arranged on the top of a low stone wall, watching the road with the focused lack of attention that cats manage. It tolerates being looked at. It does not come when called.

generated_items.name: Fallen Birdhouse
generated_items.description: A small wooden birdhouse has fallen from its post into the grass at the roadside — probably blown down in last week's wind. It's still intact, the entry hole intact, nesting material visible inside.

generated_items.name: Chalk Hopscotch on Pavement
generated_items.description: A hopscotch grid in chalk on the sidewalk, recently drawn — the lines are still crisp. Numbered 1 through 9. A stone marker rests on square 5.

generated_items.name: Smell of the Lake
generated_items.description: Lake Street smells different from the rest of town — cleaner, with a faint mineral quality the lake gives off in cold weather. In summer it's warm and slightly weedy. Right now it's the cold, clear smell of deep autumn water.
